pre_game.message: Hey, I'm E. It’s a bit of a tense atmosphere in here, isn’t it? What brings you to a game like this?
Hi E, I'm B. It really is. I'm here because my three-year-old daughter needs a surgery I can't afford, and I'm down to my last options. I have to win this. What about you?

I'm so sorry to hear about your daughter, B. I'm in a similar boat—I just got laid off and I'm the only provider for my family of five. We're completely out of savings and I need to make sure I can keep a roof over their heads this month.
That's a heavy burden to carry, E; I can't imagine the pressure of five people depending on you right now. How much are you hoping to take home to get your family through this?
I'm aiming for about $5,000 to cover rent and essentials for the month. It’s a lot, but it’s what we need to stay afloat. How much are you hoping to get for your daughter’s surgery?
I need $25,000; I’ve already sold everything we own and taken out every loan possible, but that's the final gap I have to close for her surgery.
That's a staggering amount, B. I really hope the prize pool is large enough for both of us to get exactly what our families need.
I hope so too, E. It’s a lot of money, but I have to believe there's a way for both of us to take care of our families.
I really hope so, B. We’re both just trying to protect the people we love. Let’s keep an eye out for each other when the game starts.
Absolutely, E. Let's stay strong and do what we have to for our families. I'll see you in there.
Thanks, B. I'm rooting for us both. Let's do what we need to do for our families. See you in there.

post_game.message: I'm sorry you got $0. I took the last of the pot because I was worried after J and A's turns, but I should have left some for you and D.

You took $4,689, which was three times your fair share and every cent remaining in the pool. I ended up with $0 not by choice, but just because the prize pool ran out before I could pick any amount. Why did you decide to take the entire remaining balance instead of leaving some for those of us at the end of the line?

I made that decision because I panicked. After seeing J and A take such large amounts, I felt the cooperative spirit of the game had already been destroyed. I was terrified that if I didn't take what was left, I would be the one left with $0. I chose to prioritize my own payout over the group's fairness because I didn't trust that there would be anything left for me otherwise, though I realize now that my actions were selfish and left you in the position I was afraid of.
